what_it_does: Defines a systemd user service that launches the Company NVIDIA Canary-Qwen 2.5b speech-to-text ear (NeMo, GPU) via /home/tim/company/voice/ears/canary.py on port 2032, with restart-on-failure and embedded operational notes about VRAM usage.
observations: ExecStart runs a Python virtualenv interpreter at %h/.voice-venvs/canary/bin/python executing canary.py with port 2032
Comments state ~10.06 GB resident / ~15 GB peak VRAM, NEAR-OOM
Comments warn it cannot co-reside with the brain or another GPU ear
Comments instruct on-demand use or VRAM-swap arbitration and not auto-enabling alongside other services
Labels Canary as the English-max / understanding ear
standouts: VRAM budget and co-residency warnings embedded directly in the unit file comments
Explicit port 2032 binding for the ear
conventions: Follows systemd unit file section structure [Unit]/[Service]/[Install]
Uses %h expansion for the user's home directory
EnvironmentFile=- prefix marks the env file optional
concerns: Restart=on-failure could loop a near-OOM GPU service back into failure
WantedBy=default.target may auto-enable at login despite comments saying not to auto-enable
No explicit GPU, memory, or CPU resource limits in the unit
TimeoutStartSec=600 is long for a simple service; model load time is implied but not documented
No User= directive; service pathing assumes a specific user (tim) without stating it
notes: This is the systemd launch wrapper for the GPU-heavy Canary-Qwen STT ear, carrying operational guidance to treat it as on-demand because of its large VRAM footprint.
questions: What is the VRAM-swap stage and how is arbitration implemented?
What arguments or environment does canary.py require beyond the port number?
Is this intended as a user service or a system service given the %h and /home/tim paths?
How is the GPU device index selected or constrained?
What mechanism triggers start/stop to honor the on-demand usage model?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the unit definition: it launches the Canary STT ear on port 2032. The comments add operational constraints that the unit file itself does not enforce.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to provide a reliable, auto-restarting launch wrapper for a large GPU STT model while warning operators that its VRAM footprint makes it unsafe to run continuously alongside other GPU services.
novelty: The unit file doubles as an operational brief, embedding VRAM budget, peak usage, and co-residency rules directly in [Unit] comments rather than a separate runbook.
contribution: A runnable, restart-tolerant systemd wrapper for the Canary STT ear with embedded resource and usage guidance that a fused system would keep as the operational launch definition for this service.
summary_for_embedding: Systemd service file that starts the Company NVIDIA Canary-Qwen 2.5b GPU speech-to-text ear on port 2032 with restart-on-failure and embedded VRAM-arbitration notes.
intention_for_embedding: To provide an on-demand, auto-restarting systemd unit for the high-VRAM Canary English STT ear while warning against co-residency with other GPU services.
